title: loxotrema alpestris
intro: Loxotrema alpestris, also known as the alpine wormseed, is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a vermifuge, to treat intestinal worm infestations. The plant's health benefits also include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat digestive issues and skin problems.
